faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers to what our clients ask most.

items.question: How long does company formation take?
items.answer: Most cases are completed within 5 to 10 business days once the required documents are in place.

items.question: Can foreigners own a company fully?
items.answer: Yes. Full foreign ownership is possible for many activities within the Aqaba Special Economic Zone, but some activities are prohibited, restricted, or require special approvals. We review your activity before starting to confirm the right path.

items.question: What documents are required?
items.answer: We provide a precise list based on your activity — typically passports or IDs, partner details, and an activity description.

items.question: Do the fees include government charges?
items.answer: The package fee covers our services in full; official government fees are charged separately and fully transparently.

items.question: Do you offer post-formation services?
items.answer: Yes. We support bank account opening, professional licensing, and accounting services as needed.

items.question: Do I need an existing company before registering in ASEZA?
items.answer: Not always. We review your case and clarify what is needed by activity and legal form.

items.question: Can I start procedures from outside Jordan?
items.answer: Often yes, subject to required documents, legalization, and legal conditions.

items.question: Can an existing ASEZA company's activity be amended?
items.answer: It may be possible depending on company status, activity, documents, and authority approval.

items.question: Can I import immediately after registration?
items.answer: Not always. Customs, tax, or additional approvals may still apply based on goods type.

items.question: Does an industrial project need environmental approval or inspection?
items.answer: It may, depending on activity and site, and is subject to authority decision.

items.question: Do tourism activities have different requirements from regular commercial companies?
items.answer: Yes, some tourism activities require additional sector approvals before operation.

items.question: Is company registration enough to start a real estate project?
items.answer: Usually no. Planning, building permits, and occupancy approvals may be required.

items.question: Is ASEZA.co the official ASEZA authority website?
items.answer: No. It is a private legal service by Barakat Law Firm.

items.question: Can you review a client file before submission?
items.answer: Yes. We review classification, documents, and requirements before filing within mandate scope.

items.question: Does registering the company mean I can immediately start the activity?
items.answer: Not always. The registration certificate is an essential first step, but some activities require additional approvals — such as a public-safety certificate, public-health certificate, environmental approval, or work-commencement permit — before operations can begin.

items.question: Is the income tax always 5%?
items.answer: This cannot be treated as a blanket rule. Some registered establishments may benefit from a special income tax rate depending on the activity, income source, and applicable legal exemptions and conditions.

items.question: What is the service cost?
items.answer: Contact us via WhatsApp to ask about our fees. The fee covers consultation, document preparation, application submission, and follow-up according to the mandate, with a full explanation of any post-registration requirements.

cta.title: Need to confirm if your activity can be registered?
cta.subtitle: Send us a short activity summary. We will explain the first step, required documents, and whether additional approvals may apply.
cta.secondary: Or read the registration steps first

finalCta.subtitle: Send us a brief description of your activity — we will review it and respond within one business day.

meta.title: FAQ on Company Registration in Aqaba
meta.description: Thorough answers to the most common questions about company registration in the Aqaba Special Economic Zone: registration, costs, documents, timeline, and post-registration.

faqPage.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faqPage.subtitle: Everything you need to know about setting up your company in Aqaba, organized by topic.

items.question: Is aseza.co the official website of the Aqaba Special Economic Zone Authority?
items.answer: aseza.co is a private legal service provided by Barakat Law Firm, and is not the official website of the Aqaba Special Economic Zone Authority. We are a private law firm offering company formation services in the zone — not a government entity.

items.question: How do I start the registration process?
items.answer: Get in touch to book a free consultation, where we study your activity and determine the most suitable legal structure, then handle the rest of the procedures for you until you receive your license.

items.question: Can foreigners fully own the company?
items.answer: Yes. Registering in the zone allows full foreign ownership for many activities without needing a local partner, though some activities are prohibited, restricted, or require special approvals — which we review with you before starting.

items.question: What's the difference between registering the company and registering the establishment with the authority?
items.answer: Registering the company creates the legal entity and gives it legal personality. Registering the establishment with the Aqaba Special Economic Zone Authority is the step that grants it "registered establishment" status in the zone and qualifies it for the zone's special regime. The two are separate, complementary steps, and we handle both within the package.

items.question: Does the office have to be inside the Aqaba Special Economic Zone?
items.answer: Yes. A company registered in the Aqaba Special Economic Zone must have a registered address inside the zone. However, the company's operations don't all have to run from inside the zone, and in some cases virtual or shared offices can be used. We explain the available options based on your activity type.

items.question: Can registration be completed from Amman or by proxy?
items.answer: Yes. You can authorize our firm to complete all registration procedures without needing to travel to Aqaba. We handle communication with the authority, document submission, and follow-up on your behalf under a notarized legal power of attorney.

items.question: Do I need an actual warehouse or office?
items.answer: It depends on the nature of the activity. Commercial and service activities may suffice with a registered address and no physical warehouse, especially for back-to-back trading. Activities that require actual storage or logistics operations need physical facilities. We clarify the requirements for your case.

items.question: Does an import/export trading activity need special approvals?
items.answer: It depends on the nature of the goods. General trading activities don't need additional approvals in most cases, but certain goods — such as chemicals, food, pharmaceuticals, or regulated products — require additional sector approvals. We review the nature of the goods with you before starting.

items.question: What happens if the activity is restricted or needs an additional approval?
items.answer: We explain this to you from the outset during the initial review. If the activity is possible with additional approvals, we guide you to the required path and the appropriate points of contact. If the activity is prohibited or needs restructuring, we recommend specialized consultation before starting — we don't begin formation procedures for an activity we know won't be approved.

items.question: How much does the service cost?
items.answer: Contact us via WhatsApp to ask about our fees. The fee covers consultation, document preparation, application submission, and follow-up until you receive your license.

items.question: What government fees should I expect?
items.answer: Government fees vary by activity, legal form, capital, and required approvals. We provide a full breakdown of the expected fees for your case before work begins, alongside the total quote. Our firm's fees are fixed and clear from the start, and separate from government fees.

items.question: Does every company need sales-tax registration?
items.answer: No. Sales-tax registration is not mandatory for every company; it depends on the activity type, annual sales volume, and whether sales are inside or outside the zone. We review the tax-registration requirements for your case and recommend the right path.

items.question: Are there any hidden fees?
items.answer: None at all. We clarify exactly what is and isn't included before we begin — no surprises.

items.question: What documents are required for registration?
items.answer: Typically partner ID or passport copies, an activity description, proposed company names, and proof of premises; we provide a precise list tailored to your activity.

items.question: Do I need to translate foreign documents?
items.answer: Yes. Documents issued outside Jordan require attestation and certified translation, which we recommend completing early to avoid delays.

items.question: What if a document is missing?
items.answer: We review your file in full before submission and guide you to complete anything missing, minimizing the chance of notes or delays.

items.question: How long does the registration process take?
items.answer: Usually within 5–10 business days, depending on the activity type and how complete the documents are.

items.question: What speeds up the procedures?
items.answer: Preparing documents accurately and providing them early is the fastest route; we always work to complete each step as quickly as possible.

items.question: When can I start operating?
items.answer: Receiving your license completes the registration process; depending on your activity, additional operational certificates or approvals may be required before you actually begin. We flag these to you in advance and guide you through the post-formation steps.

items.question: Can I start operating right after registration?
items.answer: Once the company is registered, your business may need additional certificates or approvals depending on the nature of its activity — such as a public-safety certificate, a public-health certificate, environmental approval, or a work-commencement permit. We explain from the outset what applies to your activity so you don't start with incomplete procedures.

items.question: Do you help with opening a bank account?
items.answer: Yes. We guide and assist you with the company bank account opening procedures after you receive your license.

items.question: Do you provide accounting services?
items.answer: Yes. We offer support with accounting and tax services as needed to help run your company smoothly.

items.question: What about specialized professional licenses?
items.answer: We help you complete any sector or professional licenses your activity requires after formation.

Activity review is an initial review and does not mean official approval.
Selecting the legal path for foreign companies depends on the parent-company documents, activity, and competent authorities.
Amending company records does not necessarily complete updates to all licenses or activity certificates.
An entity registration certificate does not necessarily mean all activity licenses are complete.
Tax and customs rules are subject to the nature of the activity, income source, and applicable regulations.
Government fees may change and are confirmed before payment.

hero.title: Company Registration Service in the Aqaba Special Economic Zone
hero.subtitle: We handle the entire formation of your Aqaba company end to end — with a clear, transparent fee and precise follow-up until you receive your license.

pricing.title: What Does the Formation Package Include?
pricing.subtitle: One all-inclusive fee for our services, with a full breakdown of what you get.
included: Initial consultation to determine the right legal structure
Company name reservation and availability check
Preparation of the articles and memorandum of association
Completing and submitting the registration application to the zone authority
Following up on official approvals every step of the way
Collecting your registration certificate and license on your behalf
Legal advisory throughout the formation period

notIncluded: Official government fees of the Aqaba zone authority
Capital and registration fees that vary by activity

pricing.note: Government fees are charged separately and fully transparently, and vary by activity type and capital; we provide a precise estimate before we begin.

govFees.title: What affects the government fees?
govFees.lead: Our fees are fixed and clear from the start. Government fees, however, vary from case to case based on several factors, and we give you a full breakdown of the expected fees before work begins.

factors.title: Type of commercial activity
factors.description: Fees vary by the nature of the activity — commercial, service, industrial, or logistics.

factors.title: Legal form of the company
factors.description: Limited liability company, one-person company, or foreign company branch — each form has its own fees.

factors.title: Registered capital
factors.description: Some fees are tied to the value of the approved capital.

factors.title: Additional approvals and licenses
factors.description: If the activity requires sector approvals such as public health, public safety, or environmental approval, those approvals carry their own separate fees.

factors.title: Number of partners and shareholders
factors.description: The ownership structure may affect some administrative fees.

govFees.closing: We send you a full breakdown of the expected government fees for your case after the initial review of your activity.

activities.title: Common activities we help establish
activities.lead: We work with a wide range of commercial activities. Below are some of the most common, noting that each case's eligibility for registration depends on the details of the activity and the approvals required.

items.title: Import and export
items.description: Forming companies that import and export goods to and from the Aqaba Special Economic Zone.
items.caveat: We review the nature of the goods and the approvals required before starting.

items.title: General trading
items.description: General trading and distribution companies inside and outside the economic zone.
items.caveat: We determine with you the products covered and any potential restrictions.

items.title: Services and consulting
items.description: Service companies of all kinds, from management consulting to professional services.
items.caveat: We review whether the service activity needs an additional professional license.

items.title: Logistics services
items.description: Shipping, storage, and logistics companies tied to the movement of goods in the zone.
items.caveat: We review warehouse and logistics-licensing requirements based on the type of service.

items.title: Foreign company branch
items.description: Registering branches of foreign companies wishing to operate in the Aqaba Special Economic Zone.
items.caveat: Branch registration requirements differ from forming a new company — we clarify the right path for your case.

activities.closing: Your activity not listed here? Get in touch — we work with a variety of activities and review each case individually.

comparison.title: On Your Own, or With Us?
comparison.subtitle: The difference between going through the procedures yourself and letting us handle them.

rows.diy: Weeks of running around and waiting
Procedures may repeat when notes arise
rows.withUs: We address any authority notes and follow through to completion

items.title: Application submission

items.title: Review & approvals
items.description: We follow the authority's review and address any notes immediately.

items.title: License delivery
items.description: Final approval is issued and we hand you your license, ready to go.

items.question: Are your fees final?
items.answer: Yes. Our fees are fixed and cover all of our services; official government fees remain separate and we explain them up front.

items.question: Do I need to attend in person?
items.answer: In most cases we handle procedures by proxy; some steps may require your signature or a notarized authorization.

items.question: What if the application is rejected?
items.answer: We review documents carefully before submission to minimize rejection risk, and address any notes until approval is granted.

items.question: Do you help after formation?
items.answer: Yes — we support bank account opening, professional licensing, and accounting services as needed.

meta.title: Your Company Registration Process in Aqaba
meta.description: A detailed, step-by-step explanation of the company registration process in the Aqaba Special Economic Zone, including the documents required and the timeline for each stage.

hero.title: Your Aqaba Company Registration Process, End to End
hero.subtitle: A detailed guide to every step — with the documents required and the expected time — so you know exactly what happens and when.

steps.title: Initial consultation
steps.description: We start with a session to understand your activity, your goals, and your expected scope of work. We discuss the available options and answer your initial questions, leaving with a clear plan for the formation path that suits you.

steps.title: Choose the legal structure
steps.description: Based on your activity, number of partners, and ownership shares, we select the most suitable legal entity with you — whether a limited liability company, a one-person company, or otherwise — and define the capital and trade name.
documents: Passport or ID copies for all partners
Three proposed company names, in order of preference

steps.title: Prepare documents
steps.description: We draft all required legal documents precisely and in line with the zone authority's regulations, and review them with you before finalizing to ensure they're free of any errors that could delay the application.
documents: Articles and memorandum of association (we prepare these)
Proof of the company's address in Aqaba
A detailed description of the business activity

steps.title: Submit the application
steps.description: We submit the complete application with all attachments to the Aqaba Special Economic Zone Authority, pay the official fees, and obtain a tracking number for the file.

steps.title: Track approvals
steps.description: We follow the file's progress with the relevant bodies step by step, address any notes or additional requests as soon as they arrive, and keep you continuously updated on your application.

steps.title: Check additional approvals by activity
steps.description: We review whether your activity requires additional approvals such as public safety, public health, or environmental approval, and clarify the path before you begin operating.

steps.title: Receive your license
steps.description: Once final approval is issued, we collect the registration certificate and license and hand them to you ready to use, guiding you through post-formation steps such as opening a bank account.

checklist.title: Get the Full Document Checklist
checklist.description: Download a checklist of every document required so you're ready before we begin.

items.question: How long does the whole process take?
items.answer: Usually within 5–10 business days, depending on the activity type and how complete the documents are.

items.question: What do I need to get started?
items.answer: To begin, ID or passport copies and a clear idea of your activity are enough — we guide you on the rest.

items.question: Can the procedures be sped up?
items.answer: Preparing documents accurately and providing them early is the fastest route; we always work to complete each step as quickly as possible.

benefits.title: Why do investors choose Aqaba?
benefits.subtitle: ASEZ offers a practical environment for companies seeking a regional base, clearer cost structures, and direct market access by sea, land, and air.

cards.title: Tax advantages
cards.text: Registered entities may benefit from a special tax framework inside the zone, depending on activity and applicable legal conditions.

cards.title: Customs treatment
cards.text: The zone has special customs treatment that may support import, export, storage, and re-export models under applicable rules.

cards.title: Port and airport
cards.text: Aqaba connects Jordan to regional and international markets through a seaport, an international airport, and road corridors.

cards.title: Strategic location
cards.text: Located on the Red Sea, Aqaba serves as a key connection point between Jordan and surrounding regional markets.

cards.title: Promising sectors
cards.text: Suitable opportunities across trade, import/export, logistics, storage, light industry, tourism, and services.

cards.title: Clearer procedures
cards.text: A dedicated registration and licensing framework helps investors understand requirements before operations begin.

suitable.title: Is Aqaba suitable for your activity?
sectors: Foreign companies seeking an entry point to Jordan or nearby markets
suitable.note: Some activities may be restricted or require additional approvals, so we always begin with activity review.

snapshot.title: Aqaba investor advantages at a glance
items: Special income-tax treatment for some registered entities under legal conditions
Special customs treatment within the zone
Seaport and international airport
Red Sea location
Opportunities in trade, logistics, tourism, and industry
Potential to serve Jordanian and nearby regional markets
snapshot.note: Income tax for some registered entities may reach 5%, depending on activity and legal exceptions.

comparison.title: Aqaba vs. Amman vs. Free Zone

options.title: Regular company in Amman
options.bestFor: Local services or activity focused on the Jordanian market without special-zone benefits.
options.limitation: Typically does not benefit from ASEZ-specific advantages.

options.title: Company in ASEZ
options.bestFor: Import/export, distribution, logistics, foreign investors, and some industrial/tourism activities.
options.limitation: Some activities may require additional approvals or certificates.

options.title: Free-zone or storage-only model
options.bestFor: Storage or re-export in specific cases.
options.limitation: May not fit every operational model or commercial activity.

international.title: For investors from outside Jordan
international.body: If you are considering establishing a company in Jordan, Aqaba may be a suitable option when your activity is linked to trade, distribution, logistics, tourism, industry, or regional expansion. We help you understand legal form, activity viability, and required documents before starting.

investor.taxNote: Not all companies are automatically required to register for sales tax. Obligations depend on activity type, goods or services, place of sale or consumption, sales volume, and applicable tax instructions. Sales-tax treatment may differ by goods/services and consumption location, so registration in the zone should not be treated as a blanket sales-tax exemption.
investor.customsNote: Goods imported into the Aqaba Special Economic Zone may receive special customs treatment under the law. However, moving goods to the rest of Jordan, selling outside the zone, or dealing with vehicles/special goods may trigger customs duties, taxes, or additional requirements depending on the case.

activityCheck.title: Is your activity permitted, restricted, or prohibited?
activityCheck.note: We therefore always start with activity review before filing, and we do not guarantee acceptance of any activity before the competent authority decision.

cards.title: Usually permitted activities
cards.text: Commercial, service, industrial, or tourism activities that do not fall under restricted or prohibited lists, subject to case-specific review before filing.

cards.title: Restricted activities
cards.text: Some activities require special conditions or approvals, such as legal services, audit, medical services, customs clearance and sea freight, financial services, insurance, tourism and travel, contracting, engineering services, air transport, mining, telecommunications, power generation, and others.

cards.title: Prohibited activities
cards.text: Some activities cannot be registered, such as weapons and explosives, radioactive or nuclear materials, environmentally harmful substances, hazardous/narcotic/toxic materials, printing banknotes or stamps, certain active biological/genetic materials, and other activities designated by competent authorities.

legalAlerts.title: Important legal notices
items: This website is a private legal service and is not the official website of the Aqaba Special Economic Zone Authority.
Published information is for general guidance and does not replace reviewing official legislation or obtaining legal advice for your specific case.
We do not guarantee approval of any application or activity; approval depends on the competent authority decision, document completeness, and legal compliance.
Issuance of a registered-establishment certificate does not necessarily mean all operating licenses or certificates are complete.
Tax and customs benefits depend on activity nature, income source, goods movement, tax registrations, and applicable regulations/instructions.
Government fees, annual fees, and any additional charges may change and are confirmed before payment.

officialRefs.title: Key official references
officialRefs.intro: For official review, you can refer to the following laws and official portals:

items.title: ASEZ Law No. 32 of 2000 (as amended)
items.description: Core legal framework for the zone, authority powers, registration, customs, taxes, and economic activities.

items.title: ASEZ Establishments Registration Regulation No. 13 of 2001 (as amended)
items.description: Eligibility, restricted/prohibited activities, registration procedures, obligations, and renewals.

items.title: ASEZ Income Tax Regulation No. 53 of 2005 (as amended)
items.description: Rules for calculating registered-establishment income, income sources, and exceptions.

items.title: ASEZ Sales Tax on Goods and Services Regulation No. 54 of 2005 (as amended)
items.description: Sales tax and special tax rules for goods and services inside the zone.

items.title: ASEZA e-Services Portal
items.description: Practical source for e-services, procedures, and official updates.
